cards.title: Can You Refuse Work During a Heatwave?
cards.body: As temperatures rise significantly, parents may wonder about their rights to keep children home from school during extreme heat. BBC News explains what protections exist for workers and families during dangerous weather conditions.

cards.title: New Drug Delays Type 1 Diabetes Onset
cards.body: A breakthrough immunotherapy now available on the NHS can give children and adults an extra three years before requiring insulin. This development offers hope for families managing type 1 diabetes.

cards.title: Can't Afford a Tutor for Grammar School?
cards.body: A parent worries their daughter won't fulfill her potential without expensive tutoring. Annalisa Barbieri explores whether parental support and encouragement matter more than professional coaching.

cards.title: Only Children Aren't Weirdos, Parents Insist
cards.body: Polly Hudson challenges persistent prejudices about only children, arguing they're not selfish or maladjusted. She celebrates the growing army of single-child families rejecting outdated stereotypes.

cards.title: Should You Give Your Baby a Pension?
cards.body: Parents are being encouraged to open self-invested personal pensions for children, raising questions about where the money comes from. Emma Beddington explores this unlikely new source of parental guilt.

cards.title: Eight-Year-Old Refused UK Passport
cards.body: A British parent living in Switzerland had her youngest child's passport application rejected despite submitting identical documents to those accepted for her other two children. The Passport Office's unexplained decision has left the family frustrated.

cards.title: ACT Therapy Helps Mothers in Pregnancy
cards.body: Rather than trying to stop difficult thoughts and emotions, ACT (Acceptance and Commitment Therapy) offers mothers a different healing path during pregnancy and postpartum. The approach helps women move beyond avoidance.

cards.title: How to Avoid Pathologizing Child Emotions
cards.body: Adults should validate young people's ordinary emotional expression rather than labeling it as pathological. This approach respects children's developmental need to experience and process normal feelings.

cards.title: Why Screens Matter in Childhood Persuasion
cards.body: Parents worry about screens, but children learn lessons in persuasion much earlier through everyday experiences. Understanding how influence works helps families navigate media consumption thoughtfully.
